hero.hero_title_1: Run Any Database Natively on SUSE Rancher Prime - Without the Compromise.
hero.hero_description: Deploy and manage 25+ Production-grade databases as native Kubernetes resources on SUSE Rancher Prime. Run production databases with full automation, governance, and portability across on-prem, cloud, edge, and air-gapped environments.

platform.heading: How KubeDB supercharges your Rancher environment
platform.description: Turn your SUSE Rancher Prime clusters into a fully governed, self-service Database-as-a-Service platform, with zero operational complexity.

cards.title: Unified DBaaS Across Any Kubernetes
cards.description: Deploy and manage databases consistently across all clusters managed by Rancher Prime, on-prem, cloud, edge, and air-gapped, without re-architecting or vendor lock-in.

cards.title: Automated Database Lifecycle on Rancher
cards.description: KubeDB Operator runs natively on top of Kubernetes clusters managed by Rancher Prime and automates provisioning, scaling, upgrades, backups, and high-availability using Kubernetes-native APIs.
features: Works with ArgoCD, Flux & Rancher tools

cards.title: Governance, Security & Sovereignty
cards.description: Rancher's centralized policy engine + KubeDB's RBAC, multi-tenancy, and backup/recovery gives you enterprise-grade control while enabling developer self-service.

suse_table.heading: Why SUSE Rancher Prime + KubeDB wins together

reasons.title: Centralized Kubernetes management
reasons.description: Rancher Prime gives you a single pane of glass for all clusters. KubeDB adds automated database operations on top.

reasons.title: Enterprise-grade security & governance
reasons.description: Combine Rancher's policy engine with KubeDB's RBAC, TLS, encryption, and multi-tenancy.

reasons.title: Run any database anywhere
reasons.description: 25+ Production databases with the same consistent experience across all Rancher-managed environments.

comparison.heading: SUSE Rancher Prime with Traditional DBaaS vs SUSE Rancher Prime + KubeDB
comparison.description: KubeDB delivers broader database support, lower cost, and true Kubernetes-native experience on your Rancher Prime platform.

rows.kubedb: 25+ engines: PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, Redis, Kafka, Elasticsearch, ClickHouse, Cassandra, Neo4j & more
No licensing fees • Enterprise support optional
Native SUSE Longhorn - replicated, snapshots, high performance
Built-in TLS + Rancher Prime RBAC & security policies
Integrated with Rancher Prime observability stack
Same manifests across all Rancher-managed clusters

benefits.heading: Why Existing SUSE Rancher Prime Customers Choose KubeDB on Rancher Prime

items.title: Significantly Lower TCO
items.description: Only pay for the amount of Managed Database Memory(GB) while maximizing your existing Nutanix investment.

items.title: Developer-First Agility
items.description: Databases become Kubernetes objects. Enable self-service provisioning, GitOps workflows, and faster iteration, all while running alongside your microservices on NKP.

items.title: Enterprise Security & Operations
items.description: Built-in TLS, encryption, automated backups with Stash, high availability, and seamless integration with Nutanix microsegmentation and NKP security policies.

items.title: Full Nutanix Performance
items.description: Nutanix CSI provides blazing-fast persistent storage. Combine with NKP's monitoring and data services for complete lifecycle management.

items.title: Hybrid & Multi-Cloud Ready
items.description: Use identical manifests across on-premises NKP, NC2 on public clouds, or any CNCF Kubernetes, avoiding vendor lock-in common with traditional DBaaS.

items.title: Proven at Enterprise Scale
items.description: Trusted by organizations worldwide for production databases. Rapid innovation with commercial support, while protecting your Nutanix infrastructure investment.

architecture.heading: KubeDB runs natively on SUSE Rancher Prime
architecture.description: The KubeDB Operator sits directly on top of your Rancher-managed Kubernetes clusters, leveraging Rancher's centralized management, policy engine, and multi-cluster capabilities.

layers.label: KubeDB Platform
layers.desc: Web Console • Envoy Gateway • Hub/Spoke Model • Policy Engine

layers.label: Kubernetes Services
layers.desc: ArgoCD • Open Cluster Management • Cert Manager • Prometheus • Grafana

capabilities.heading: Production databases. Fully automated on Rancher.
capabilities.description: 25+ Database engines with HA, scaling, backup, TLS, monitoring, and GitOps support

database_support.description: 25+ Database engines managed with Kubernetes-native workflows

features.text: Run anywhere: on-prem, cloud, or edge
